key_highlights: US-Iran tensions escalate with reported collapse of talks and new Hormuz target date, driving oil prices up and weighing on equities.
AI investment frenzy continues with massive capital commitments from Big Tech (Alphabet $80B, Berkshire $10B) and IPO filings from Anthropic and SpaceX.
Fed Chair Warsh makes first hires, including a 'Project 2025' author, signaling potential policy shift; bond market sees biggest drop in two weeks on rate-hike bets.
Yen weakens to 160 against dollar after BOJ hints at rate hike, prompting official warnings.
Eurozone inflation hits 3.2% due to Iran war energy costs, while US manufacturing recovery remains muddled.
Private credit exotics boom with hedge funds targeting Ferrari owners; short seller Andrew Left convicted of securities fraud.
Retail sector mixed: Macy's raises outlook on sales gains, but global fashion retailer closes all stores after 33 years.

sectors.trend: Oil prices jump sharply after US-Iran talks collapse, with Brent likely testing $90+; Kuwait plans to expand global storage; Hormuz alternatives only partial fix.
sectors.implications: Higher energy costs will feed into inflation globally, pressure central banks to maintain or hike rates, and benefit oil producers but hurt consumers and transport sectors.

sectors.trend: AI spending accelerates with Meta, Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet committing massive capex; Berkshire makes $10B AI bet; Anthropic and SpaceX file for IPOs; Marvell stock surges on AI hype.
sectors.implications: AI infrastructure buildout is a dominant theme, driving demand for chips, data centers, and networking. However, spending backlash is emerging (Anthropic), and valuations are frothy. Palo Alto Networks dips on earnings despite acquisitions.

sectors.trend: Deutsche Bank may need more credit provisions; UniCredit increases Commerzbank stake to 34.4%; BNP manager buys SMR after selloff; JPMorgan sees earnings supercycle; Partners Capital caps redemptions.
sectors.implications: European banks face credit risk from energy crisis, while US banks remain optimistic. Private credit and alternative assets continue to grow, but liquidity risks appear (redemption caps).

sectors.trend: Macy's raises outlook on four straight quarters of sales gains; Victoria's Secret turnaround driven by bra sales; Target opens $367M distribution center; but a global fashion retailer closes all stores.
sectors.implications: Consumer spending is bifurcated: discount and essential retailers thrive, while mid-tier and luxury face pressure. Inflation and higher rates weigh on discretionary spending.

sectors.trend: Treasuries see biggest drop in two weeks on Fed rate-hike bets; Yen falls to 160 vs dollar; Eurozone inflation rises to 3.2%; Fed Chair Warsh hires 'Project 2025' author; bond market faces 'doom loop' of debt and inflation.
sectors.implications: Bond yields are rising on rate-hike expectations, pressuring equities. Yen weakness may trigger intervention. The new Fed chair's hawkish tilt could further tighten financial conditions.

sectors.trend: Mortgage rates reverse course and rise; HELOC and home equity loan rates remain low but rate shopping matters; British millennials struggle with homeownership.
sectors.implications: Rising mortgage rates will cool housing demand, especially in already unaffordable markets. Home equity remains a bright spot for consumers with existing homes.

key_themes: Geopolitical risk: US-Iran conflict escalation and oil price spike
AI investment supercycle: Big Tech and Berkshire pour billions into AI infrastructure
Central bank policy divergence: Fed hawkish, BOJ hints at hike, RBI pauses
Inflation resurgence: Eurozone 3.2%, energy-driven, complicates rate decisions
Private credit and alternative assets boom: Hedge funds target exotic assets, redemptions capped
Retail bifurcation: Winners (Macy's, Victoria's Secret) vs losers (fashion retailer closure)
Currency volatility: Yen at 160, rupiah at record low, Indonesian stocks slump
Regulatory and legal: Short seller Andrew Left convicted, new tariffs proposed by Trump

risk_assessment.risk_factor: US-Iran military escalation and oil supply disruption
risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase exposure to energy stocks and commodities; hedge with oil futures or energy ETFs; reduce exposure to airlines and consumer discretionary.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Fed rate hike expectations and bond market selloff
risk_assessment.mitigation: Short duration bonds; favor floating rate instruments; reduce leverage in portfolios; consider TIPS for inflation protection.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: AI spending bubble and valuation froth
risk_assessment.mitigation: Take profits on high-flying AI stocks; diversify into value and defensive sectors; monitor cash burn and ROI of AI capex.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Currency crisis in emerging markets (Yen, Rupiah, Indonesian stocks)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce EM equity and bond exposure; hedge currency risk; focus on exporters benefiting from weak local currencies.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Private credit liquidity crunch (redemption caps, fraud conviction)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid illiquid private credit funds; favor liquid alternatives; monitor redemption gates and leverage levels.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Global inflation resurgence due to energy costs
risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase commodity exposure; overweight energy and materials; underweight long-duration bonds and growth stocks.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy energy stocks and oil ETFs on geopolitical risk premium
opportunities.rationale: Oil prices likely to stay elevated due to Iran conflict and Hormuz disruption; producers benefit from higher prices and potential supply constraints.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate AI infrastructure plays (semiconductors, data centers, networking)
opportunities.rationale: Massive capex from Big Tech and Berkshire validates long-term demand; companies like Marvell, Nvidia, and Ayar Labs are key enablers.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in high-yield savings and money market accounts for cash reserves
opportunities.rationale: Rates up to 4.10% APY provide safe, liquid returns amid market volatility and rising rates.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider buying beaten-down EM stocks selectively (India, Korea, Taiwan)
opportunities.rationale: India's tax cuts and RBI pause support growth; Korea and Taiwan have strong tech exports; valuations are attractive after recent selloffs.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce equity exposure and increase cash or short-term Treasuries
defensive_moves.rationale: Geopolitical uncertainty, rising rates, and frothy valuations suggest near-term downside risk; cash provides optionality.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge portfolio with gold or gold ETFs despite recent slip
defensive_moves.rationale: Gold remains a safe haven amid Middle East tensions and inflation; pullback offers entry point.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Underweight consumer discretionary and retail, especially luxury and mid-tier
defensive_moves.rationale: Rising rates, inflation, and potential recession will pressure spending; Macy's strength is an exception, not the rule.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid private credit and illiquid alternative funds
defensive_moves.rationale: Redemption caps and fraud cases highlight liquidity and operational risks; stick to liquid, regulated investments.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets will remain volatile with a negative bias due to escalating US-Iran conflict, rising oil prices, and Fed rate-hike fears. Equities may correct 5-10% from current levels, while energy and commodities outperform. Bond yields will continue to rise, pressuring growth stocks. The yen and EM currencies face further weakness.
market_outlook.long_term: AI investment supercycle and potential de-escalation in Middle East could drive a recovery in H2 2026. However, structural inflation, higher-for-longer rates, and geopolitical fragmentation pose headwinds. Earnings supercycle thesis (JPMorgan) may play out if inflation moderates and capex drives productivity gains. Emerging markets with strong fundamentals (India, Korea) offer long-term value.
key_catalysts: Fed meeting and Warsh's policy signals
BOJ rate decision and yen intervention
Q2 earnings season (especially Big Tech and energy)
Anthropic and SpaceX IPO pricing and demand
EU energy crisis aid package details
US manufacturing PMI and jobs data
watch_list: Oil price (WTI, Brent) and Hormuz Strait developments
10-year Treasury yield and Fed funds futures
USD/JPY at 160 level and BOJ intervention
Bitcoin price below $67,000 and Coinbase stock
Trump tariff announcements on India, Switzerland
